books.title: The Strange Case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: A London lawyer investigates the relationship between a brilliant scientist and a misshapen misanthrope.

books.title: The Special Correspondent
books.author: Jules Verne
books.summary: A Parisian news reporter is assigned to travel across Asia by train and report on the sights, experiences, and interesting characters along the way.

books.title: Struggles and Triumphs
books.author: P. T. Barnum
books.summary: The world’s greatest showman tells the story of his life.

books.title: Eminent Victorians
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: Four witty and biting biographies of Victorians that take aim at the moral superiority of their subject’s time.

books.title: A Little Princess
books.author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
books.summary: A young girl discovers a life of unexpected challenges when she goes to live at a London boarding school.

books.title: Uneasy Money
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: When Lord Dawlish unexpectedly comes into an inheritance, he travels from London to New York to try to give it away.

books.title: The Mule-Bone
books.author: Langston Hughes
books.summary: A guitarist and a dancer compete for the same woman’s affection.

books.title: Khaled
books.author: F. Marion Crawford
books.summary: A powerful genie is condemned to live the life of a man, unless he can find true love.

books.title: Just William
books.author: Richmal Crompton
books.summary: An eleven-year old English boy gets involved in a continuous series of amusing adventures to the exasperation of his long-suffering family.

books.title: The Strange Case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: A London lawyer investigates the relationship between a brilliant scientist and a misshapen misanthrope.

books.title: A Personal Record
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: Conrad recounts his childhood in Poland, his sailing experiences, and the work on his debut novel.

books.title: The Sea Mystery
books.author: Freeman Wills Crofts
books.summary: The dogged detective is called in to investigate the grisly contents of a cask dragged up from the bottom of the sea.

books.title: The Public and Its Problems
books.author: John Dewey
books.summary: A discussion of the political philosophy of a democratic society.

books.title: The End of the World
books.author: Geoffrey Dennis
books.summary: A philosophical reflection on how the end of the world would unfold based on hypothetical variables and relevant knowledge and historical context.

books.title: Hudibras
books.author: Samuel Butler
books.summary: A knight and his squire set forth for adventure and love.

books.title: Evelina
books.author: Fanny Burney
books.summary: A young woman navigates the absurdities of 18th-century English society, fends off various suitors, and attempts to establish her true parentage.

books.title: The Moon Maid
books.author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
books.summary: A young man travels to the moon with a group of companions and discovers that it’s inhabited; in subsequent centuries, reincarnations of the same man deal with an invasion of the Earth by the malevolent inhabitants of the Moon.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Vsevolod Garshin
books.summary: A collection of Garshin’s translated short stories and novellas ordered by date of publication.

books.title: The Four Men
books.author: Hilaire Belloc
books.summary: Hilaire Belloc takes a pilgrimage across his beloved home English county of Sussex writing as four distinct personalities, with a wide variety of songs, stories, and digressions.

books.title: On a Chinese Screen
books.author: W. Somerset Maugham
books.summary: A collection of short vignettes about the people and places W. Somerset Maugham encountered during his travels along the Yangtze River in 1919–20.
